member.name: M:Tmds.DBus.Protocol.Reader.ReadHandle``1
member.summary: Reads a Unix file descriptor handle.
member.typeparam: The type to read. If is , the method returns a disposed instance without consuming the underlying handle.
member.remarks: A handle can only be read once. Use to avoid consuming the handle.
member.exception: The file descriptor is not present in the message.

member.name: M:Tmds.DBus.Protocol.Reader.ReadHandleRaw
member.summary: Reads a Unix file descriptor handle as a raw IntPtr.
member.remarks: A handle can only be read once. To skip reading a handle, call ReadHandle<SkipSafeHandle>(), which will return a disposed instance without consuming the underlying handle. The handle is still owned (i.e. Disposed) by the .
member.exception: The file descriptor is not present in the message.

para.b: Constructing a VariantValue for writing with :

remarks.para: The following types support implicit conversion to : , , , , , , , , , , , . These conversions may also be performed explicitly using the corresponding static methods: , , , , , , , , , , , .
Array, struct, and dictionary values can be strongly-typed using the , (and generic variants), and classes. These classes implicitly convert to .
For arrays, static Array methods (and related overloads) allow creating a directly from array or instances of , , , , , , , , , , , , , and .
For structs, static Struct methods (and related overloads) allow creating a directly from instances for its fields.
To wrap a value in a variant container, use . For Unix file descriptors, use .
returns the value type. For , use to determine the array element type. For , use and to determine the dictionary key and value types. returns the number of items for , , or types.
Use typed getters to retrieve values: , , , , , , , , , , , .
For structs, use to access individual elements by index.
For arrays, use to access individual elements by index, or to obtain a T[] array.
For dictionaries, use to access individual entries by index, or to obtain a .
To unwrap a variant container, use . For Unix file descriptors, use .

member.name: P:Nerdbank.Streams.Sequence`1.MinimumSpanLength
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum length for any array allocated as a segment in the sequence. Any non-positive value allows the pool to determine the length of the array.

remarks.para: Each time or is called, previously allocated memory is used if it is large enough to satisfy the length demand. If new memory must be allocated, the argument to one of these methods typically dictate the length of array to allocate. When the caller uses very small values (just enough for its immediate need) but the high level scenario can predict that a large amount of memory will be ultimately required, it can be advisable to set this property to a value such that just a few larger arrays are allocated instead of many small ones.
The in use may itself have a minimum array length as well, in which case the higher of the two minimums dictate the minimum array size that will be allocated.
If is , this value may be automatically increased as the length of a sequence grows.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.Streams.Sequence`1.AutoIncreaseMinimumSpanLength
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the should be intelligently increased as the length of the sequence grows.
member.remarks: This can help prevent long sequences made up of many very small arrays.

member.summary: Gets this sequence expressed as a .
member.returns: A read only sequence representing the data in this object.

member.summary: Gets the length of the sequence.

member.param: The position of the first element that has not yet been processed. This is typically after reading all elements from that instance.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.Sequence`1.Append(System.ReadOnlyMemory{`0})
member.summary: Adds an existing memory location to this sequence without copying.
member.remarks: This *may* leave significant slack space in a previously allocated block if calls to follow calls to or .

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.Sequence`1.Dispose
member.summary: Clears the entire sequence, recycles associated memory into pools, and resets this instance for reuse. This invalidates any previously produced by this instance.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.Sequence`1.Reset
member.summary: Clears the entire sequence and recycles associated memory into pools. This invalidates any previously produced by this instance.
